30th April, 2010 Statutory Instruments 105
IN EXERCISE of the powers contained in sectionjifty-five of the
Information and Communication Technologies Act, 2009, and in
consultation with the Authority, the following Regulations are
hereby made:
PART!
PRELIMINARY
Title I. These Regulations may be cited as the Information and
Communication Technologies (National Numbering Plan)
Regulations, 2010.
Interpretation 2. In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires
"allocated" means the status of a block assigned to a resource
user for a particular purpose;
"allocated for migration only" means the status of a block
allocated for a temporary period during migration from
one numbering scheme to another;
"allocation" means the broad definition of categories of
telephone numbers and codes determined by the Authority
in the National Numbering Plan, according to various
classifications of services, resource users, and applications
to which different
numbering groups are applied;
"allocation certificate" means a formal document of the
allocation of a block to a resource user for a particular
purpose;
"annual numbering report" means a report produced by the
Authority on an annual basis that summarises the current
position regarding the national numbering plan;
"annual numbering return" means information supplied by a
resource user annually to the Authority under regulation
12;
"Authority" means the Zambia Information and
Communication Technology Authority continued under
section four of the Act;
"code block" means a sequence of contiguous
numbers used as a basis for allocating codes to resource
users;
"country code" means the combination of one, two or three
digits that identifies a specific country or countries in an
integrated numbering plan or a specific geographic area;
"National Numbering Plan" means a document issued by the
Authority that specifies a structure which designates
specific numbers or number ranges and the use to which
106 Statutory Instruments 30th April, 20 I 0
they may be put and sets out policies and rules for the
allocation and use of numbers in Zambia;
"resource user" means a licensee who is eligible to be
assi ed numbers or codes under these Regulations, or a
gn
third party re-seller of numbering related services, as
determined by the Authority; and
"short code" means short, generally memorable three to five
numbers that allow end-users access to a wide range of
services.
Application 3. These Regulations apply to the allocation and use in Zambia
of numbers in the National Numbering Plan administered by the
Authority.
PART II
NATIONAL NUMBERING PLAN
Administration 4. The Authority shall be responsible for the primary assignment
of numbering of numbers to resource users in accordance with the allocation
resource
categories identified in the National Numbering Plan.
Application 5. (1) A resource user shall apply for the assignment or
for reservation of numbering resources in Form I set out in the First
assignment Schedule.
(2) An application for the ·assi ment of numbers shall, except
gn
with the approval of the Authority, be made at least
sixty days before the planned in service date.
(3) The Authority shall determine an application for the
assi ment of numbering resources within a period of thirty days
gn
from the receipt of the application.
(4) The Authority may request for additional information from
an applicant as the Authority may consider necessary for purposes
of these Regulations in Form II set out in the First Schedule.
(5) In counting the thirty days referred to in sub-regulation (3),
no account shall be taken of the period in which the Authority
requests for more particulars or information under sub-regulation
( 4) . Consideration of application for assi ment of numbers
gn

sections.heading: (!)TheAuthority shall reject an application for an assignment
sections.text:
of numbering resources where---
(a) the applicant has not used the previous assignment of
numbers in an efficient manner or has mismanaged
the assi ed numbers; or
gn
(b) the application does not meet the requirements of these
Regulations.
30th April, 2010 Statutory Instruments 107
(2) The Authority shall, where it rejects an application, partially
grants an application or attaches any conditions to an allocation of
numbers, inform the applicant in Form III set out in the First
Schedule.

sections.heading: (!)The Authority may approve an application under these Assignment
sections.text:
Regulations with or without any conditions. of numbers
(2) The Authority shall assign the numbering resources in
proportion to the needs of the applicant and in a transparent and
equitable manner, with priority in the assignment of the available
numbering resource being given on the basis of the order of receipt
of the applications.
(3) The Authority may, in consultation with an applicant, make
an alternative assignment of numbers on the application that meets
the applicant's requirements.
(4) The Authority may determine, on a case by case basis, the
quantity of number assignments required for particular resource
users, based upon demonstrated end-user demand.
(5) The Authority shall assign the numbers in an opened block
prior to the numbers in any un-opened block.
(6 ) The Authority may, upon the request ofa resource user, assign
separate blocks of numbers to the resource user, where the
Authority determines that the assignment would enhance market
competition and not unduly diminish the non discriminatory
availability of numbering resources.
(?)The Authority shall, upon the grant of an assignment, issue a
resource user with an allocation certificate in Form IV set out in
the First Schedule.

sections.heading: (I) A resource user shall use the numbers assigned for the Terms and
sections.text:
conditions
purpose specified in the application.
for use of
(2) Subject to such modifications as the Authority may determine
primary
on number portability, any numbers assigned to a resource user assignment
shall remain under the control of the resource user.
(3) The Authority may, at the time of assigning the numbering
resources, or at any time thereafter, impose conditions on the use
of the numbering resources where it considers that it is necessary
or reasonable to do so in the interests of the National Numbering
Plan:
(4) Any conditions imposed under sub-regulation (3) shall be
consistent with these Regulations and relate only to the use and
management of the assigned numbering resources.

sections.heading: (I) The Authority may reserve numbering resources where-- Reservation
sections.text:
of numbering
resources.
108 Statutory Instruments 30th April, 2010
(a) a resource user does not wish to be identified by the public
at the time of the application;
(b) the reason for the application is commercially-sensitive
at the time of the application;
(c) the reason for the application should not be divulged prior
to an application for the allocation;
(d) a matter in respect of which the numbering resources are
required has not been finalised; or
(e) a resource user anticipates that an application will be made
for numbering capacity.
(2) The Authority shall, where it makes a reservation of any
numbering resources under this regulation, determine an
application for the assi ment of the reserved numbering resources
gn
in accordance with regulation 6.
(3) A resource user may apply for the reservation of numbering
resources in Form I set out in the First Schedule where -
(a) the resource user has applied for a licence under the Act;
or
(b) aroute for mi ating numbers from another block or code
gr
needs to be identified.
(4) The Authority shall, within a period of thirty days from the
receipt of an application under sub-regulation (2),' determine the
application and inform the applicant of its decision.
(5) Except for the purpose for which, and to the person by whom,
the reservation of numbering resources is made under this
regulation, where a reservation of numbering resources is made,
the numbering resources shall not be available for assi ment to
gn
any other person for the period of the reservation.
( 6) The reservation ofnumbering resources under this regulation
does not guarantee that a corresponding assignment shall be made.
(7) Unless longer reservation is needed for the effective
management of the National Numbering Plan, the limit for
reservations for fixed and mobile ranges of numbers shall be six
months.
(8) A reservation may be renewed with the approval of the
Authority.
(9) The Authority may terminate a reservation awarded to a
person who has applied for a license under theA ct where the person
does not meet the criteria for the assignment of numbers within
such period as the Authority may determine.
(I 0) The Authority shall make public all applications for
reservations in the same manner as for number allocation, except
where an applicant requests otherwise.
30thApril, 2010 Statutory Instruments 109
I0 . A resource user shall notify the Authority, other resource Notification
users and all the institutions involved in the administration of the by resource
user
National Numbering Plan of the dates on which the resource user
activates the assigned numbers and codes within the time-scales
agreed with the other resource users.
II . (I) The Authority shall withdraw the assigned numbers from Withdrawal
a resource user where-- of numbers
(a) the resource user misuses, hoards or deals with the
numbers in an illegal manner;
(b ) the resource user contravenes theA ct or these Regulations;
or
(c) the public interest so requires.
(2) The Authority may, in consultation with other stakeholders
and any end-user who is likely to be affected by the withdrawal,
withdraw any number in use by a resource user.

sections.heading: (I )A resource user shall, within thirty days of the end of the Annual
sections.text:
numbering
calendar year, submit an
return
annual numbering return to the Authority in Form V set out in the
First Schedule.
(2)An annual numbering return submitted under sub-regulation
(1) shall accurately reflect the status of the numbering resources
assigned to the resource user by the end of the prior calendar year.
(3) The Authority shall consolidate the data collected from the
annual numbering returns submitted under this regulation in a non
commercially sensitive form in the annual nu_mbering report .
I 3. (I) The Authority shall annually publish, in a daily Publication
newspaper of general circulation in Zambia and on its website, an of annual
numbering
annual numbering
• report
report containing information relating to-
(a) the numbering resources allocated by the Authority;
(b) the recipients of the numbering resources;
(c) the conditions for use of the numbering resources which
do not relate to any confidential or commercially­
sensitive data; and
(d) the status of each resource indicating whether it is free,
reserved, assigned or unavailable and the period of
unavailability.
(2) The Authority shall update on its website the information
referred to in sub-regulation (I) at least every six months.

sections.heading: (I) A resource user shall maintain a directory ofall telephone Maintenance
sections.text:
numbers assigned to the resource user's subscribers, with the of telephone
directory by
exception of pre paid service customers for which listings resource
user
IJO Statutory Instruments 30th April, 2010
information is not practically available.
(2) A resource user shall, upon request by any other resource
user or directory publisher, provide a full list ofnumbers maintained
by the resource user.
(3)A n end-user may request a resource user not to publish the
end-user's telephone number or to avail it for any directory
publication or inquiry.
(4) A resource user or directory publisher shall not publish a
telephone number without the approval of the subscriber, except
where the publication ofs uch information is authorised under any
other law.
PART III
SHORT CORDS AND OTHER NUMBERS
Designation JS. (!) The Authority may, in accordance with the National
of short codes Numbering Plan, determine national uniform short codes to be
used by all or any class of resource users in order to access
emergency or inquiry services.
(2) The National Numbering Plan shall include distinctions of
Type A, Type B and Type C
short codes to be associated with the various types of resource
user and services, in a manner consistent with the requirements of
the National Numbering Plan.
Carrier identification
codes

sections.heading: (!)A resource.user may apply to theAuthority in Form I set
sections.text:
out in the First Schedule for a carrier identification code within
the appropriate range that the resource user wishes to be assi ed
gn
and in line with the designations in the National Numbering Plan.
(2) The Authority may assi a resource user a different code
gn
to the one applied for where it determines that the assi ment
gn
applied for or the preference of the applicant shall-
(a) violate the National Numbering Plan;
(b) not be consistent with these Regulations;
(c) unduly erode, or lead to under-utilisation oft he numbering
resources;
(d) cause interference; or
(e)b ring si ificant and unfair competitive advantage to any
gn
person.

(3) The Authority shall, where there is competition for a
particular carrier identification code, organise a transparent
and equitable lottery
under which the code in question shall be awarded. Prescribed fees

DECLARATION
I/we declare that all the particulars and information provided in this application are complete,
correct and true and I/we agree that in the event that any of the said particulars and information
provided is found to be untrue or fraudulent, the assignment/reservation will be revoked.
I/we agree that in the event of the revocation of the assignment/reservation, any fee paid to the
Authority in respect of the same shall be forfeited.I/we declare that in the event that the nature of
my/our business changes, or I/we no longer carry out operations in terms of the assignment/
reservation.

sections.heading: Registration fees are one-off fees to be paid to the Authority at the lodging in oft he

sections.heading: The assignee shall pay annual fees as shown in the table.
sections.text:
• The subscriber number (C) fee set out in the table shall be the applicable· fee for the
purpose of determination of the annual numbering fee formula, where C= 1 fee unit.
122 Statutory Instruments 30th April, 20 I 0
ANNUAL NUMBERING FEE FORMULA

sections.heading: For resource user's individually assigned number blocks. the payable
sections.text:
annual fees shall be calculated as per equation below;
FA =B x C, where
a. F A is the annual numbering fee to be paid by resource user.
b. B is the total number block assigned, allocated to the resource user
c. C is the fee for each subscriber number in the assigned number
block allocated to the resource user,
